title: Understanding Angular Momentum Conservation
text: The principle of conservation of angular momentum states that the total angular momentum before an event must equal the total angular momentum after the event, provided no external torques act on the system.

title: Calculate Initial Angular Momentum
text: The initial angular momentum of the system is the angular momentum of the boy running tangent to the merry-go-round. Use the formula: $$L=I \omega$$, where I is the moment of inertia and ω is the angular velocity.

title: Calculate Boy's Initial Angular Momentum
text: The boy's initial angular momentum can be calculated using $$L=m v r$$, where $$m$$ is the mass of the boy, v is his velocity, and $$r$$ is the radius of the merry-go-round.

title: Calculate Final Angular Momentum
text: After the boy jumps on, the total angular momentum is the sum of the angular momentum of the boy and the merry-go-round. Use the formula: $$L_{total}=I_{total} \omega_{final}$$.

title: Calculate Total Moment of Inertia
text: The total moment of inertia after the boy jumps on is the sum of the moment of inertia of the merry-go-round and the moment of inertia of the boy. Use $$I_{total}=I_{merry}-go-round+m r^2$$.

title: Calculate Final Angular Velocity
text: Using the conservation of angular momentum, set the initial angular momentum equal to the final angular momentum: $$L_{initial}=L_{final}$$. Solve for $$\omega_{final}$$.
